docs.title: Non‑Parent Caregiver Authorization – In‑a‑Box
docs.text: A portable, low‑cost outreach kit that lets volunteers help Texas families fill out, print, and notarize FM‑34‑109 (Authorization Agreement for Voluntary Non‑Parent Caregiver) on the spot.

docs.title: ADD QR CODE TO THE DOCASSMEBLE INSTANCE
Project Overview
docs.text: To make the FM-34-109 easy to complete for parents to protect their children.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Order the hardware and print the QR‑code & one‑pagers.
  2. Reach out to venues with the form letter.
  3. Schedule your event, recruit volunteers.
  4. On the day of the event, be friendly, help the best you can, and know you are helping make more children safe.

docs.title: Physical-Kit Checklist
docs.text: Item Suggested product / spec Qty Notes All‑in‑one printer with email‑printing HP DeskJet 4255e (Wi‑Fi, AirPrint, Instant Ink) – https://a.co/d/0929M8wk 1 Keep a spare cartridge & an extra tray of paper in the box. Paper 8.5×11 in, 20‑lb, white 1 ream (500 sheets) Store flat to avoid jams. Ink HP 67 Black + HP 67 Color (or Instant Ink subscription) 2 each (spare) Replace before each event. Wi‑Fi / Hotspot Mobile hotspot (e.g., Verizon Jetpack, AT&T Nighthawk) 1 4G/5G data plan with at least 5 GB/mo. QR‑code holder Acrylic sign holder (2 × 8 in) – https://a.co/d/0aMJjqup 1 Insert the QR‑code printout (see Section 3). Box / tote Sturdy rolling toolbox or heavy‑duty tote 1 Label “Non‑Parent Caregiver Kit”. Pens Box of low‑cost ballpoint pens (blue/black) 1 (≈30 pcs) Keep one pen per participant. Power strips 2‑outlet surge‑protected strips with long cords 2 Plug printer, hotspot, laptop, etc. Table 4‑ft folding table (or any flat surface) 1 Optional if venue provides a table. Chairs Lightweight folding chairs 3 For volunteers / participants. Notary public (Texas) Certified Texas notary – on‑site or on‑call via video notarization 1 per event Must have a current commission & seal. Optional Texas lawyer Family‑law attorney (pro‑bono) for Q&A 0‑1 per event Not required for notarization. Interpreter (multi‑language) Bilingual volunteer or on‑demand phone app (e.g., Google Translate) As needed Spanish is mandatory; add others per community. Laptop / tablet For printing & displaying QR‑code 1 Can be a phone with email‑print capability. Sign‑in sheet Simple paper log (name, phone, email, consent) 1 For follow‑up & data tracking (HIPAA‑safe). Sanitizing wipes Alcohol‑based wipes 1 pack Clean printer & surfaces between participants.

docs.title: Proposed budget
docs.text: Item Approx. Cost (per kit) Notes HP DeskJet 4255e printer $120 Include 2 extra cartridges ($30). Paper (500‑sheet ream) $6 Ink cartridges (spare) $30 Mobile hotspot (device + 5 GB/mo) $45 (first month) Power strips (2) $12 Acrylic sign holder $15 Box/tote $20 Pens (30 pcs) $5 Table (if purchased) $35 Chairs (3) $30 One‑pager printing (50 pcs) $10 QR‑code generation (free) $0 Misc. (sanitizing wipes, zip ties) $5 Total per kit ≈ $353

docs.title: FREE HELP – TX FM‑34‑109 (Non‑Parent Caregiver Authorization)
Why you might need this form
docs.text:
• To name a trusted adult who can make medical, school, and legal decisions for your child if you become unable to do so.
  • It’s required by schools, doctors, and many agencies.
  • No cost – we’ll help you fill it out, print it, and have it notarized today.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
1. Scan the QR code below or use the link on your phone to open the online form.
  2. Fill out the information with our volunteer’s assistance (Spanish interpreter available).
  3. Print the completed form, sign, and have a Texas notary public notarize it on the spot.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• A completed, notarized FM‑34‑109 that you keep.
  • Copies for your doctor, school, and any other agency that asks.

We’re here to protect your children. Ask a volunteer any question!

docs.title: Request to host form letter
docs.text:
Subject: Request to Host a Free “Non‑Parent Caregiver Authorization” Help Booth at [Venue Name]

Dear [Venue Contact / Community Services Manager],

My name is ________, and I volunteer with the “Non‑Parent Caregiver – In‑a‑Box” project, a free service that helps Texas families complete, print, and notarize the state‑required FM‑34‑109 Authorization Agreement for Voluntary Non‑Parent Caregiver.

We are reaching out to request permission to set up a small assistance booth at [Venue Name] on [Proposed Date(s)] from [Start Time] to [End Time]. Our goal is to:

  • Provide on‑site help for parents to name a trusted adult who can make medical, school, and legal decisions for their children if the parents become incapacitated.
  • Offer the service at no cost, with a Texas‑licensed notary present to notarize each completed form.
  • Supply participants with copies of the notarized form for schools, doctors, and other agencies.

What we will bring:

  • A portable all‑in‑one printer with Wi‑Fi hotspot, paper, pens, and a power strip.
  • A bilingual volunteer team (English/Spanish) plus a professional interpreter as needed.
  • An accredited Texas notary public (and, optionally, a volunteer attorney for legal questions).
  • Signage and QR‑code displays so participants can start the form on their phones.

Space requirements: a 4‑ft table, three chairs, and an electrical outlet (or we can use a battery‑powered hotspot). We will set up and clean up ourselves and will follow all venue health‑ and safety guidelines.

Our service aligns with [Venue’s] mission to support vulnerable families and improve community health outcomes. We would be honored to partner with you to make this valuable resource available to the families you serve.

Please let us know if the proposed date works for you, or if there is an alternative time that would be better. I am happy to discuss any logistical details or answer any questions you may have.

Thank you for considering our request. I look forward to the possibility of collaborating with [Venue Name] to protect Texas children and families.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]

[Title – e.g., Volunteer Coordinator, Non‑Parent Caregiver Project]

[Organization (if any)]

Phone: _______

Email: _______

Website (if any): ____

docs.title: AYUDA GRATUITA – TX FM‑34‑109 (Autorización de Cuidador No‑Parental)
¿Por qué necesita este formulario?
docs.text:
• Para nombrar a un adulto de confianza que pueda tomar decisiones médicas, escolares y legales por su hijo si usted no puede.
  • Lo exigen escuelas, doctores y muchas agencias.
  • Sin costo – le ayudamos a completar, imprimir y notarizar el formulario hoy mismo.

docs.title: Cómo funciona (3 pasos fáciles)
docs.text:
1. Escanee el código QR a continuación o use el enlace en su teléfono para abrir el formulario en línea.
  2. Complete la información con la ayuda de nuestro voluntario (intérprete de español disponible).
  3. Imprima el formulario completado, fírmelo y haga que un notario público de Texas lo notarie en el momento.

docs.title: ¿Qué recibe?
docs.text:
• Un FM‑34‑109 completo y notariado que usted conservará.
  • Copias para su doctor, escuela y cualquier otra agencia que lo solicite.

¡Estamos aquí para proteger a sus hijos! Pregunte a cualquier voluntario.
